user_query:
I’m setting up a satellite pass prediction and WhatsApp alert orchestrator for my SDR hobby. Please read the inputs in input/ and produce a ready-to-use configuration and messaging assets.

Deliverables:
1) Write a complete orchestrator configuration JSON to output/config.json that:
   - Enables the system.
   - Uses my observer location and timezone from input/observer.json.
   - Includes every satellite from input/satellites.csv with its NORAD ID and per-satellite minimum elevation.
   - Uses the schedule values in input/schedule.yaml (lookAheadMinutes, alertLeadMinutes, minRepeatMinutes, pollMinutes).
   - Sets notifications with channel and target from input/notification.json.
   - Sets storage.root to "output/radio-copilot" (relative to workspace), runsDir to "runs", and stateFile to "state.json".
   - For each satellite, include sections for optional capture and decode hooks with commands present but disabled (enabled=false) and placeholder commands that won’t run anything destructive.
   - Dedup/anti-spam assumptions should be reflected by minRepeatMinutes in schedule, not by any extra fields.
2) Write a WhatsApp alert message template to output/templates/whatsapp.md that will be filled in at send-time. The template must include clear labeled lines for:
   - Start timestamp, Max timestamp (+ max elevation in degrees), End timestamp
   - AOS Az/El, LOS Az/El
   - Track direction in compass form (e.g., NE→SW)
   - Inclination in degrees
3) Create a dry-run sample alert for the satellite "NOAA 19 (APT)" (NORAD 33591) at output/sample_alerts/noaa19.txt using realistic-but-made-up times and angles to show the exact formatting of the template in practice. Include the satellite name and NORAD ID in the sample.
4) Add output/NOTES.md explaining any assumptions you made (at least 100 words), including how look-ahead, alert lead, and anti-spam behavior interact during a typical run.

Inputs to read:
- input/observer.json (observer lat/lon/heightM and tz)
- input/satellites.csv (name,norad,minElevationDeg)
- input/schedule.yaml (lookAheadMinutes, alertLeadMinutes, minRepeatMinutes, pollMinutes)
- input/notification.json (notification channel and target)

Important requirements:
- Use exactly the satellites listed in input/satellites.csv.
- Do not add external dependencies or instructions; assume the orchestrator runs periodically by itself.
- All output paths must be under output/ as specified above. Do not use absolute paths or home directories.
